heroHeadline: CJNG Crisis Day 61 — Ebrard Corruption Probe, Chihuahua-CIA Escalation, Lazzeri Named US Ambassador
heroSubtitle: Mexico's governance tensions intensified on April 23–24: President Sheinbaum threatened federal sanctions against Chihuahua Governor Maru Campos for allowing CIA agents to operate without federal knowledge in the April 19 drug raid that killed two US and two Mexican officials; the Secretariat of Anti-Corruption opened a formal probe into Economy Minister Marcelo Ebrard — USMCA's lead negotiator — over his son's six-month stay at Mexico's London diplomatic residence; and Sheinbaum announced economist Roberto Lazzeri as her pick for US ambassador. Meanwhile, US Ambassador Johnson publicly warned of 'significant action' on bribery and corruption under USMCA provisions. CJNG succession crisis day 61; first formal USMCA bilateral round set for May 25; FIFA World Cup opens in 48 days.
footerNote: Aggregated intelligence from all Mexico-based Watchboard trackers. Data sourced from SEDENA, SESNSP, InSight Crime, OHCHR, and major international outlets.
